mnemonics.title: REGEX APPLICATIONS
mnemonics.content: Real Engineers Generate Excellent Xtracts Using Patterns: Real-world validation, Extracting data, Grammar/compilers, Efficient parsing, Xtreme text processing, URL parsing, Lexical analysis, Input validation, Pattern matching, Syntax highlighting

mnemonics.title: FLOW of Lexical Analysis
mnemonics.content: Filter → Lex → Organize → Wrap: Filter tokens using regex, Lexical analyzer generates tokens, Organize syntax, Wrap into parse tree

mnemonics.title: VALIDATE with Regex
mnemonics.content: Verify All Legal Inputs Directly And Test Everything: Email formats, Phone numbers, ZIP codes, Dates, URLs, ASCII patterns, Text formats

mnemonics.title: GREP Steps
mnemonics.content: Global Regex Extraction Process: 1. Define pattern 2. Read files 3. Execute match 4. Print results

acronyms.usage: Remember different regex implementations across programming languages
Recall regex standardization across UNIX systems
Core concept identifier in pattern matching
Link between regex and compiler construction

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a spinning carousel that can make 0+ full rotations (empty ride allowed)
Picture a railroad switch directing trains to different tracks (a|b paths)
Visualize a telescope adjusting focus: {n} fixed lens, {m,n} zoom range, * infinite view
See a vending machine where [A-Za-z] dispenses letter cans and [0-9] gives number bottles

analogies.analogy: Like a barcode scanner - quickly identifies predefined patterns in text streams
Similar to a factory sorting machine - uses regex as filters to categorize raw text into tokens
Acts like passport control - regex patterns check if data format is legally acceptable
Works like a mining sieve - regex filters out unwanted text and captures valuable data nuggets
